objects.name: TextBox 7
objects.text:
TULIP LINE CRM 2nd Year
Lucky Draw and Redeem Campaigns 2025 - 2026

objects.name: TextBox 10
objects.text:
Findings
Average block rate remained at 8.28% through Jan 2025
In March 2026, LINE gained 4,408 new friends
The lower block-rate trend suggests better traffic quality: the x2 points and redemption-led value exchange is attracting more purchase-intent users than the previous lucky draw model.

objects.name: TextBox 29
objects.text:
TULIP LINE TRAFFIC SOURCES - LINE GAIN FRIENDS
(Period: March 26)

objects.name: TextBox 24
objects.text:
Note
Add Friends traffic sources (Add Friend icon, Chat room button, Search, Share contact, Official Accounts list, LINE VOOM, Home tab, LINE Login, etc.) have very low individual volumes and have therefore been consolidated under “Other” for simplified reporting and clearer analysis.

objects.name: TextBox 6
objects.text:
Findings
LINE Lap was the dominant acquisition channel, contributing 3,428 new users making it the clear growth engine and contributing roughly four-fifths of total new friends in March. LINE Lap performance increased 1,575 users or 85%.
Although user blocks Although user blocks increased from 1,252 to 1,414 in line with the higher acquisition volume, so the more important signal is whether registered-user and active-user conversion

objects.name: TextBox 6
objects.text:
Findings
Redemption volume improved to 923 items from 429 redeeming users, indicating that the March x2 points push and added reward choices helped bring more members into the redeem journey.
Since campaign launch in August 2025, 1,118 users have redeemed rewards, which means redemption is growing but still concentrated in a relatively small portion of the base.

objects.name: Rectangle 17
objects.text: From 429 Users Who Redeem Items on March 26

objects.name: TextBox 23
objects.text:
Findings
Among all 1,118 redeemers, 865 users (77%) came from redeem-only behavior, while 253 users (23%)  also joined lucky draw.
However, since the lucky draw campaign launch in August 2025, only 1,978 users have joined lucky draw.

1 x คูลเลอร์ล้อลาก
21 x ผลิตภัณฑ์ทิวลิป

objects.name: TextBox 7
objects.text:
Recommendations

Introduce Premium Rewards : Offer higher-value or exclusive items to incentivize high-balance users to burn their points.

Drive Aspirational Engagement : Use these premium rewards to motivate the majority of users (the 1-50 points group) to actively collect and redeem points.

Implement 'Earn & Burn' Campaigns : Launch limited-time campaigns to stimulate users to accumulate more points and actively exchange them for rewards.

objects.name: TextBox 16

Findings
Majority of points balance still be “ร้านขายเครื่องดื่มชงแบบซุ้มหรือเคาน์เตอร์” group, which has earned 7,665 users in March 26 with 4% growth from last month. 
Total point balance remaining were from 17,536 users comprising with registered users of 23,077 users.
The average growth of each group users are increasing around 4% from last month, which was the same .

objects.name: Rectangle 15
objects.text: Calculated as 76% from Total Registered users on Loyalty Program

objects.name: TextBox 29
objects.text:
TOTAL TULIP LINE POINTS BY RANGE 
(Period: March 26)

objects.name: Rectangle 3
objects.text:
Findings
The majority of users (96%) hold only 1–50 points, with 82% having just 1–10 points.
Very few users accumulate higher point balances (above 50 points).
This indicates limited point accumulation and highlights an opportunity to encourage stronger engagement and point collection behavior.

objects.name: TextBox 6
objects.text:
Findings
In March, the campaign experienced a notable increase in new user registrations, with a cumulative increase of 739.
The strongest new-register audience is female and concentrated in the 30-39 age band, indicating a clear demographic center of gravity.

Findings
As of March 2026, most of users is female and the biggest chunk fall within the age range of 30 to 39 at 31.51% from total users 22,338.
followed by minor group of female age 20-29 and 40-49 with approximate 20% each.

objects.name: TextBox 29
objects.text:
TULIP LINE RICH MENU ENGAGEMENT
(Period: March  26)

objects.name: TextBox 12
objects.text:
Findings 

 Loyalty program Rich Menu engagement rose to 11,983 clicks, up 2,146 from last month, which shows stronger navigation activity inside the LINE ecosystem.

objects.name: Rectangle 2
objects.text:
Findings 
 ร้านขายเครื่องดื่มชงแบบซุ้ม หรือเคาน์เตอร์ is the No.1 segment (42%), making it the primary registered group.
 Followed by ผู้บริโภค (20%) as the second largest segment.

objects.name: TextBox 10
objects.text:
Findings 
มากกว่า 1 ครั้งต่อสัปดาห์ is the No.1 segment (37%)
This is followed by 1 ครั้งต่อสัปดาห์ (32%)

objects.name: TextBox 13
objects.text:
Findings
The largest group of customers bought around 1-2 pouches per purchase.
Brand only able to penetrate small shops than medium or large-scale shops.
Bulk buyers with over 50 pouches per purchase were also featured in the campaign.

objects.name: TextBox 10
objects.text:
Findings 
“อื่นๆ” is the No.1 response (35%), indicating a wide range of alternative brands in the market.
โกโก้ ดัทซ์ (20%) and โกโก้ริช (20%) follow closely as strong secondary competitors.

objects.name: TextBox 12
objects.text:
Findings

Content: "ให้คะแนน X2" (x2 points)
Achieved the highest open rate despite a smaller delivery volume, showing a strong ability to convert delivery into immediate opens. 
The reward-accumulation framing created urgency and a clear, universal benefit. 
Successfully drove higher attention and participation because the value is instant and easy to understand.

Content: "เพิ่มของรางวัลใหม่" (new rewards added)
Delivered at a high volume but with a lower open rate compared to x2 points. 
Indicates that expanded reward options are relevant, but not as compelling as an immediate earning incentive. 
Performance depends on whether users are actively interested in redeeming, rather than creating broad, instant appeal.

Content: "แจกเมนูเด็ด" (menu / lifestyle content)
Suggests that lifestyle or informational content is less effective at driving immediate curiosity. 
Engagement tends to rely on specific user interest (e.g., food/menu preference) rather than a strong promotional hook.

objects.name: TextBox 14
objects.text:
Summary

LINE OA continued strong growth, reaching 73.7K friends, driven mainly by LINE platform campaigns, while new user acquisition remained heavily reliant on in app LINE traffic.
March 2026 showed strong top-funnel growth, with 4,408 new friends added and clear momentum from LINE Lap.
The quality of acquisition improved after the shift from lucky draw to x2 points and redemption-led value, as reflected in healthier block-rate behavior and stronger redemption activity.
The most valuable audience is now clear: female users aged 30-39 and small beverage-shop operators, especially ร้านขายเครื่องดื่มชงแบบซุ้ม หรือเคาน์เตอร์ .
Lead with earning (x2 points) to drive attention, then follow with rewards to convert, and use content as support , structure campaigns as a funnel, not one-off sends.
March campaign performance shows a clear difference in user motivation. Among the three campaign themes launched this month, x2 points generated the highest open rate at 17.57%, outperforming added rewards at 14.23% and menu communication at 14.93%.
This indicates that users are most responsive to campaigns built around immediate and tangible benefit. In this case, earn more now proved to be a stronger trigger than either more options to redeem or program/menu update.
Always communicate immediate value (“what do I get now?”)  this is the key driver behind higher opens, engagement, and conversion.
Instead of treating campaigns separately, design a flow:
x2 points → drive opens + earn behavior 
new rewards → convert to redemption 
menu/content → sustain engagement 
   This turns campaigns into a funnel
Overall, Tulip CRM is succeeding at reaching the right audience, but it now needs to convert that audience more effectively from add-friend into register, earn, redeem, and repeat behavior.

objects.name: TextBox 15
objects.text:
Recommendations

Prioritize point multiplier campaigns as the main monthly traffic driver, because March shows they generate the strongest immediate response and are the clearest value proposition for users.
Sequence campaigns more intentionally:
Start with earn messaging such as x2 points to drive opens and attention.
Follow with redeem messaging to convert that attention into reward usage.
Use menu or feature-update messaging only as support, not as the lead campaign.
Use block rate as a quality KPI, not just a support metric. March suggests better acquisition quality, so future campaigns should be judged not only by how many users they bring in, but also by how many users stay and continue engaging.
Reduce over-reliance on one acquisition source. LINE Lap is performing well, but the brand should strengthen secondary sources so monthly growth is less dependent on one channel.
Expand premium or aspirational rewards for high-value users current point accumulation is too low, and the reward structure isn’t strong enough to build long-term loyalty.
